summary: The U.S. reportedly suspected Israel of plotting to assassinate Iran's top nuclear negotiators during April talks, prompting emergency protective measures including an Iranian parliamentary speaker Ghalibaf's plane making an emergency landing in Mashhad after alleged Israeli jet incursions. Simultaneously, Israel claims 83% of Gaza's 'yellow line' tunnels destroyed and eliminated a Hezbollah operative exiting an underground tunnel in Lebanon's Ali Taher ridge. Explosions rocked Syria's Deir ez-Zor and Damascus amid reports of Syrian government demolitions of Shiite villages and clashes with Israeli forces in Daraa.
key_points: U.S. intelligence warned Iran that Israel may have planned to assassinate negotiators Ghalibaf and Araghchi during April nuclear talks, leading to extraordinary protective measures. (Source: @asafroz/50942)

Iranian parliamentary speaker Ghalibaf's plane made emergency landing in Mashhad after Iranian security detected alleged Israeli fighter jets entering Iranian airspace near Iraq border. (Source: @asafroz/50942)
Israeli Defense Minister Katz stated 83% of tunnels in Gaza's 'yellow line' area have been destroyed. (Source: @yediotnews25/132224)
IDF Egoz unit eliminated a Hezbollah operative who exited an underground tunnel in Lebanon's Ali Taher ridge using air strike. (Source: @idf_telegram/24500)

Israeli warplanes struck Barishit town in Lebanon; drone strike reported near Nabatieh hospital; artillery shelling hit Beit Yahoun. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/374066, @yediotnews25/132218, @redlinkleb/29520)

Explosions reported in Syria's Deir ez-Zor near Iraq border and in Damascus with casualties; Syrian government demolishing Shiite villages, two Shiite youths killed in Daraa fighting Israel. (Source: @SharghDaily/169408, @yediotnews25/132207, @naya_foriraq/80622)
European nations reportedly accept Iran imposing fees on ships transiting Hormuz Strait, urging non-discriminatory application. (Source: @asafroz/50936, @rasedal3ado138e/374059)
IDF arrested suspect in car-ramming attempt near Beit Ummar, West Bank; investigating soldier suspected of filming bound Palestinian in Gaza. (Source: @idf_telegram/24503, @inon_yttach/9253)

sections.executive_summary: The U.S. reportedly suspected Israel of plotting to assassinate Iran's top nuclear negotiators during April talks, prompting emergency protective measures including Ghalibaf's plane making an emergency landing in Mashhad after alleged Israeli jet incursions. Simultaneously, Israel claims 83% of Gaza's 'yellow line' tunnels destroyed and eliminated a Hezbollah operative exiting an underground tunnel in Lebanon's Ali Taher ridge. Explosions rocked Syria's Deir ez-Zor and Damascus amid reports of Syrian government demolitions of Shiite villages and clashes with Israeli forces in Daraa.

points: Six U.S. B-52 bombers departed RAF Fairford in the UK after being deployed since March as part of force buildup related to Iran; Pentagon has not confirmed if withdrawal signals reduced posture. (Source: @asafroz/50932)

NYT reports U.S. suspected Israel attempted to assassinate Iranian negotiators Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf and FM Araghchi during April talks; U.S. asked regional states to warn Iran, leading to Pakistani fighter escorts for Ghalibaf's Islamabad trip. (Source: @asafroz/50942)

Ghalibaf's plane made emergency landing in Mashhad after Iranian security received intelligence that two Israeli fighter jets entered Iranian airspace near Iraq border intending to attack the aircraft; delegation traveled eight hours by land to Tehran. (Source: @asafroz/50942)
Iran's FM spokesman said U.S.-sponsored Bahrain meeting aims to conceal America's destabilizing policies and lawless behavior in the region. (Source: @presstv/196577)

European countries reportedly agree ships transiting Hormuz Strait will pay fees to Iran and Oman; EU accepts this as unavoidable but urges non-discriminatory application. (Source: @asafroz/50936, @rasedal3ado138e/374059)

Iran FM Araghchi met SCO Secretary-General Nurlan Yermekbayev in Tehran; UN Secretary-General Guterres called Araghchi expressing condolences for Khamenei's death and discussing regional developments including Hormuz Strait. (Source: @presstv/196579, @SharghDaily/169412)
Defense Minister Israel Katz announced 83% of tunnels in Gaza's 'yellow line' area have been destroyed. (Source: @yediotnews25/132224)

IDF Egoz unit under Division 36 identified and eliminated a Hezbollah operative exiting an underground tunnel shaft in Ali Taher ridge using IAF strike; forces prevent militants from exiting subterranean infrastructure. (Source: @idf_telegram/24500)

Israeli Channel 14 claims exposure of complex intelligence infiltration within IRGC structure, alleging targeted killings in 39-day and 12-day wars relied on human intelligence from inside IRGC passed to Mossad. (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74646)

IDF and Defense Ministry arrested suspect in car-ramming attempt near Beit Ummar, West Bank, at 'Mitzadot Yehuda' crossing after joint pursuit by Military Police and IDF. (Source: @idf_telegram/24503)
IDF identified soldier suspected of involvement in filming bound Palestinian in Gaza; command investigation underway, involved personnel to be handled per findings. (Source: @inon_yttach/9253)
Defense Minister Katz stated 83% of tunnels in Gaza's 'yellow line' area destroyed. (Source: @yediotnews25/132224)
IDF investigating soldier suspected of filming bound Palestinian in Gaza; command investigation ongoing. (Source: @inon_yttach/9253)

Car-ramming attempt reported near Beit Ummar village in Etzion Brigade area; no casualties; IDF pursued suspects and arrested one at Mitzadot Yehuda crossing. (Source: @yediotnews25/132209, @idf_telegram/24503)
Group of approximately eight Breslov Hasidim entered tombs in a village (likely West Bank) at night; at 04:30 reported fear for lives; two located so far. (Source: @inon_yttach/9244)
Al-Manar correspondent reported Israeli warplanes struck Barishit town in southern Lebanon. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/374066)
Lebanese reports indicate Israeli drone strike near hospital in Nabatieh, South Lebanon. (Source: @yediotnews25/132218)
Artillery shelling reported on Beit Yahoun in southern Lebanon. (Source: @redlinkleb/29520)
Warplanes observed flying toward southern Lebanon; highest alert issued. (Source: @redlinkleb/29525)

IDF Egoz unit eliminated Hezbollah operative exiting underground tunnel in Ali Taher ridge; forces prevent militants from exiting subterranean infrastructure in security zone. (Source: @idf_telegram/24500)
Lebanese reports of incident with Israeli forces; details under review. (Source: @pkpoi/143114)
Explosion heard in Deir ez-Zor, Syria near Iraq border per news sources. (Source: @SharghDaily/169408, @naya_foriraq/80620)
Reports of casualties in explosion in Damascus, Syria. (Source: @yediotnews25/132207)

Syrian government reportedly demolishing Shiite villages; two Shiite youths from Nubl and Al-Zahra killed in Daraa province clashes with Israeli occupation forces defending southern Syria. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80622)
Bloomberg reports several European countries agree ships passing through Hormuz Strait will pay fees to Iran and Oman. (Source: @asafroz/50936)
European nations reportedly accept Iran imposing Hormuz transit fees as unavoidable, urge non-discriminatory application. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/374059)
Iran FM Araghchi met SCO Secretary-General Nurlan Yermekbayev in Tehran. (Source: @presstv/196579)

Ghalibaf's plane made emergency landing in Mashhad after Iranian security detected alleged Israeli fighter jets entering Iranian airspace near Iraq border; delegation traveled eight hours by land to Tehran. (Source: @asafroz/50942)
Israeli Defense Minister Katz claims 83% of tunnels in Gaza's 'yellow line' area destroyed. (Source: @yediotnews25/132224)

IDF Egoz unit eliminated Hezbollah operative exiting underground tunnel in Ali Taher ridge using IAF strike; forces prevent militants from exiting subterranean infrastructure. (Source: @idf_telegram/24500)

Israeli warplanes struck Barishit town; drone strike near Nabatieh hospital; artillery shelling on Beit Yahoun in southern Lebanon. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/374066, @yediotnews25/132218, @redlinkleb/29520)

Explosions reported in Syria's Deir ez-Zor near Iraq border and Damascus with casualties; Syrian government demolishing Shiite villages, two Shiite youths killed in Daraa fighting Israel. (Source: @SharghDaily/169408, @yediotnews25/132207, @naya_foriraq/80622)

European nations accept Iran imposing Hormuz Strait transit fees on ships, urge non-discriminatory application; Iran FM meets SCO chief in Tehran. (Source: @asafroz/50936, @rasedal3ado138e/374059, @presstv/196579)
sections.threat_assessment: The alleged Israeli plot to assassinate Iranian negotiators during active diplomacy represents a dangerous escalation that could trigger direct state-on-state conflict. Simultaneous Israeli kinetic operations in Lebanon (Ali Taher ridge, Barishit, Nabatieh) and Gaza tunnel destruction claims indicate a multi-front pressure campaign. Syrian instability deepens with explosions in Deir ez-Zor and Damascus alongside reported demographic engineering targeting Shiite communities. The Hormuz Strait fee imposition risks maritime escalation if commercial vessels resist payment. These converging vectors suggest high probability of miscalculation or intentional expansion of hostilities.
sections.regional_response: Iran has implemented extraordinary protective measures for leadership including military escorts and travel changes following U.S. warnings. European states appear to be acquiescing to Iran's Hormuz fee demands while urging non-discrimination, reflecting reluctance to confront Tehran. Lebanon's Hezbollah faces continued degradation of subterranean infrastructure in southern Lebanon. The Syrian government's reported demolition of Shiite villages and clashes with Israeli forces in Daraa indicate Damascus may be pivoting away from Iranian-aligned constituencies. Regional actors are hedging amid uncertainty over U.S. commitment and Israeli intentions.
sections.intelligence_notes: The NYT-sourced revelation of U.S. warnings to Iran about Israeli assassination plots provides rare insight into U.S.-Israel intelligence sharing and divergence during diplomacy. The emergency landing in Mashhad after alleged Israeli jet incursion, if confirmed, would constitute a significant airspace violation. Israel's public claim of 83% tunnel destruction in Gaza's 'yellow line' lacks independent verification but signals operational focus on subterranean threats. Channel 14's claimed exposure of IRGC infiltration, if authentic, represents a major counterintelligence coup. The convergence of explosions in Deir ez-Zor and Damascus warrants monitoring for Israeli strike patterns or internal Syrian dynamics.
